comment: Declarative expected end-state for anon and authenticated grants on schema public. Absence of anon entries is stated explicitly — it is not implied. All fields must be present; empty arrays mean zero grants expected.

table_grants.comment: Direct object-level grants on public schema tables. Source: information_schema.role_table_grants.
table_grants.anon_explicit_none: anon holds zero table grants on any public schema table. Any grant is a CONTRACTS.md §12 violation.

routine_grants.comment: EXECUTE grants on public schema routines. Source: information_schema.role_routine_grants. anon and authenticated must match execute_allowlist.json exactly. service_role lists integration-only routines (not app-user callable).
routine_grants.anon_explicit_none: anon holds zero routine grants except CONTRACTS.md §12 controlled exceptions: resolve_form_slug_v1 and submit_form_v1 (Build Route 10.8.1). Any other EXECUTE grant to anon is a violation.

authenticated.authority: Build Route 10.8.11O1 — owner-only archived workspace restore; EXECUTE to authenticated

service_role.authority: Build Route 10.8.11I7 — send-invite-email Edge Function; EXECUTE to service_role only (REVOKE PUBLIC)
Build Route 10.8.11O — retention-lifecycle Edge Function; EXECUTE to service_role only (REVOKE PUBLIC, authenticated)

sequence_grants.comment: USAGE grants on public schema sequences. Source: information_schema.role_usage_grants.
sequence_grants.authenticated_explicit_none: authenticated holds zero sequence grants. All writes go through SECURITY DEFINER RPCs.

default_acl.comment: Operator-owned default privileges in schema public. Source: pg_default_acl WHERE defaclrole NOT LIKE supabase_%. Empty means private-by-default is enforced — no default grants exist for any new objects.
default_acl.operator_owned_explicit_none: pg_default_acl contains zero entries for operator-owned roles (postgres and any migration-owned role) granting privileges to anon or authenticated in schema public. This enforces private-by-default per CONTRACTS.md §13 and GUARDRAILS §11.
default_acl.platform_roles_excluded: Roles matching supabase_% are platform-managed and explicitly excluded from this truth file. They are logged by ci_anon_privilege_audit.ps1 but not enforced here.

migration_grant_allowlist.comment: Authoritative allowlist for the migration-grant-lint gate. Any GRANT in supabase/migrations/**/*.sql to anon or authenticated not present here fails the gate. Comments in migration files are not a control mechanism.

superseded_grants.comment: Historical grants that exist in migration text but are explicitly revoked by a later migration. The lint skips these only if the superseding migration file exists on disk. This section does not legitimize the grants — it records known migration history. Adding an entry here requires the superseding migration to be present and unconditional.

entries.rationale: Grant predates migration-grant-lint gate. Revoked unconditionally in 00009. End-state is CONTRACTS.md §12 compliant. Retro-editing 00007 is forbidden per migration discipline.

Grant in 003 was an authoring error — table grants to authenticated violate CONTRACTS.md §12. Revoked unconditionally in 006. End-state is compliant. Retro-editing 003 is forbidden as it is already pushed to cloud.

rules.description: anon must have zero table grants on all public schema tables
authenticated may have SELECT and UPDATE on user_profiles only — no other table grants
anon must have zero routine grants on all public schema routines

authenticated routine grants must match execute_allowlist.json exactly. Exception: current_tenant_id() is granted to authenticated to support RLS policy evaluation per QA ruling 2026-03-19 (10.8.5 remediation).
anon and authenticated must have zero sequence grants
pg_default_acl for operator-owned roles must contain zero entries granting privileges to anon or authenticated in schema public
